title: mdrcel oracle adapter output contract
description: Normative shape/types/nullability of the single JSON object each oracle adapter (run.py / run.mjs) writes to stdout. The BEHAVIOURAL contract (invocation, single-write+flush serialization, the ok/error/exit tri-state, same-machine determinism) is defined by HLD 'mdrcel Oracle Adapters' section 3 and CANNOT be expressed here; on any discrepancy between this schema and section 3.2/3.4 annotations the schema governs, but section 3 governs behaviour. additionalProperties is intentionally true: the consuming harness (benchmark/src/oracle.rs) reads a subset of fields with serde and MUST NOT reject an otherwise-valid envelope that carries an extra field (sibling O3 / no deny_unknown_fields).

contract_version.description: Schema/contract revision constant. Emitted as the FIRST field on EVERY path (success, found-nothing, and the catchable-failure envelope) so each result row is self-describing (parent brief). v1 = 1; increments only if HLD section 9 reconciliation amends this schema. Declared here and emitted by both adapters, but DELIBERATELY ABSENT from `required`: the current harness OracleResult (benchmark/src/oracle.rs) does not yet model this field, and the harness schema_conformance test asserts schema-required is a subset of OracleResult's serialized fields. Listing it in `required` would break that green cross-team gate before the harness side adds the field. It remains a contract-mandated constant via HLD section 3.2 (behavioural), not via `required`.

oracle_version.description: Resolved upstream extractor version, or null iff the library could not be imported / its version was unreadable (sibling O3 — the harness types this Option<String> and structurally depends on null being legal).

title.description: Document title verbatim from the extractor, or null. Not coerced ''->null.

text.description: Primary comparison surface. NEVER null — '' when nothing was extracted ('found little' is a valid ok:true result, not an error). Guaranteed valid UTF-8 by the adapter's well-formed primitive (HLD section 3.3); a nullable text would silently defeat the never-null guarantee (Bug-E2 trap).

html.description: Extracted HTML where the tool produces it for free (Readability .content; null for Trafilatura v1). Never scored; wire-fidelity only.

word_count.description: The adapter's own simple word count over `text`. Informational only — the harness recomputes via its single tokenizer and ignores this. null on the failure path. JSON has no unsigned integers; a plain integer.

canonical_url.description: Tool-reported canonical URL, or null. Faithful-to-tool; never scored.

ok.description: true iff the adapter ran and produced a (possibly empty) result; false on the adapter's own failure path (co-occurs with non-zero exit). Invariant: ok == (error == null). The harness types this non-Option bool and branches the tri-state on it.

error.description: Human-readable failure message on the failure path; null when ok:true (invariant ok == (error == null)).
